reasoning: The C&I loans series (TOTCI) has historically exhibited a steady random walk with positive drift, supported by a 6-month trend measuring +1.07 billion per week. This provides a baseline probability >0.50 for future values to exceed the exact starting condition at the forecast date. However, recent weeks have shown a mild dip (-10B over the past 3 weeks), and there are modest signs of credit tightening (e.g. 15.9% of lenders reported tighter standards in recent surveys, alongside softening loan demand). We forecast near-term positive growth slightly above 0.50. Per the explicit forecasting instructions, uncertainty MUST increase over the horizon, meaning probabilities for distant outcomes should be adjusted closer to 0.50. Thus, we degrade the probability mathematically downwards from 0.58 (1 week) to 0.55 (4 weeks) to 0.52 (13 weeks). There is also a small risk of a structural break or panel shift at the start of the year (as seen in a previous January), giving additional grounds to pull the January 24 estimate closer to a coin flip.
